rationale.R1.1: Holistic opinion requires reconstructing the thesis (selective risk minimization via mirror descent) and the contribution claim (accelerated rate on non-smooth losses); essential baseline for any assessment.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes precise mathematical claims — O(1/T) vs O(1/sqrt(T)), Lipschitz assumption in Theorem 1 vs stated non-smooth generality, step-size schedule — whose internal consistency must be checked for any substantive opinion.
rationale.R1.3: A single empirical claim (30% fewer epochs on CIFAR-10 at coverage 0.7) is offered as evidence; whether it adequately supports the broader convergence claims is central to a rounded view.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly claims a first-of-kind position ('first mirror-descent procedure…'); assessing the delta against projected SGD and prior selective-risk work is necessary to evaluate that claim.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking whether the mathematical claims and internal proof logic are correct (e.g., does Theorem 1 actually justify the O(1/T) headline rate given its own assumptions), or whether the contribution is novel and significant relative to existing selective-risk and mirror-descent literature?
